node.title: Uber phone screen - Combinations of dishes from the menu

topic.title: Uber phone screen - Combinations of dishes from the menu

post.content:
Position: Software Engineer

Given a menu card and an amount, how many dishes can I afford to eat/drink at the restaurant. Return all such combinations of dishes from the menu, which I can pay for in given amount. 

```
Explanation:

Menu:
Noodle  $12.87
Rice    $8.23
Soup    $5.76
Coke    $3.12
Pizza   $10.89

Amount:  $30

Output: 
{{Noodle,Rice,Soup,Coke}, 
{Noodle,Pizza,Soup}, 
{Pizza,Rice,Soup,Coke}, 
{Noodle,Noodle,Coke}, 
{Noodle,Rice,Rice}, 
{Rice,Rice,Rice,Coke}, 
{Soup,Soup,Soup,Pizza},
{Coke,Coke...9x},
{Pizza,Pizza,Coke,Coke}}

As,  
Noodle+Rice+Soup+Coke = 29.98
Noodle+Pizza+Soup = 29.52
Pizza+Rice+Soup+Coke = 28 ...so on

Note: The amount we aggregate should be closest to the given amount i.e in this case $30
```
backtracking. I feel given those inputs memoization would not make a difference.
I think it\'s a variation of coin change problem. But instead of finding exact change,find the last item which if added causes the cost of bill to overflow.
[@vishnushiva](https://leetcode.com/vishnushiva) I don\'t know how closest can be defined ?. Probably can\'t afford to buy a cheapest dish ? and time complexity is 2^n I believe

Simple DFS should work here. Was memoization expected? The memo can grow large since, we need to store partial combinations from varying amounts between $0-30. Is there a limit on the number of items on the menu card? Any opportunities to encode partial combinations in binary? Or small arrays?
